decks.notes: This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, using cards like Fleeting Spirit, Fatal Push, and Parhelion II to control the board and generate value. The key strategy is to ramp up mana quickly with cards like Godless Shrine and Mycosynth Gardens, while disrupting the opponent with removal spells like Vanishing Verse and Thoughtseize. Monument to Endurance and Iron-Shield Elf provide additional protection for the combo pieces, allowing the player to overwhelm the opponent with powerful flying constructs. The goal is to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push for disruption and removal, while also incorporating cards like Monument of Endurance and Iron-Shield Elf for protection and resilience. The deck aims to control the game early on with efficient removal spells and then transition into its combo pieces to seal the victory. It also includes lands like Fleeting Spirit and Brightclimb Pathway for additional utility and versatility in gameplay.

The main strategy of this deck revolves around utilizing the Greasefang and Parhelion II cards to create a powerful combo that overwhelms the opponent. By controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize, while also ramping up mana with cards like Brightclimb Pathway and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth, the deck aims to set up its win condition quickly. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Guardian of New Benalia and Monument to Endurance provide additional support and protection for the combo pieces.

The focus of this deck is to control the game using a variety of spells and powerful creatures, with Niv-Mizzet Reborn as the centerpiece. The deck utilizes a diverse mana base to support its multi-colored spells and features versatile cards like Bring to Light and Kolaghan's Command. It also includes removal spells like Supreme Verdict and Abrupt Decay to handle opposing threats. The goal is to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent by efficiently controlling the board and generating card advantage, ultimately leading to a victory with the help of Niv-Mizzet and other powerful finishers.

This deck focuses on quickly establishing a strong board presence through low-cost creatures like Llanowar Elves and Badgermole Cub, while using disruptive cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Skyclave Apparition to control the opponent's board. The deck also utilizes cards like Collected Company and Elspeth, Storm Slayer to generate additional value and finish off opponents quickly. The mana base is optimized for consistency and includes a mix of white and green sources to support the aggressive game plan. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with efficient creatures and disruptive spells.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. By using mana ramp and efficient creatures like Llanowar Elves and Skyclave Apparition, the deck aims to quickly establish a board presence and apply pressure. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria disrupt the opponent's game plan, while cards like Collected Company provide additional threats. By combining efficient creatures, disruptive elements, and combat tricks like Elspeth, Storm Slayer, the deck seeks to go wide and overpower the opponent before they can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing various counterspells, removal spells, and card draw effects. The deck aims to stall the game until it can establish a dominant board presence with powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer. The inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion provides an additional source of value and power. By utilizing efficient removal and control tools, this deck aims to outlast and outplay opponents, ultimately securing victory through superior card advantage and board control.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, board wipes, and card draw to outvalue and outlast the opponent. It includes cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, Supreme Verdict, and Dovin's Veto to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building up resources with Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive. The inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion adds an extra layer of control and consistency to the deck. Overall, the key concept of this UW Control deck is to maintain control of the game through careful resource management and disruption.

This aggro Selesnya deck focuses on quickly deploying creatures and disrupting opponents with cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. The deck also uses cards like Collected Company and Skyclave Apparition to maintain board presence and interact with the opponent's board. The inclusion of Abandoned Air Temple and Boseiju, Who Endures provides additional utility and protection, while cards like Michelangelo's Technique and Ouroboroid offer powerful late-game options. The mana base is optimized for consistent early plays with Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic, supported by dual lands like Razorverge Thicket and Temple Garden.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly play low-cost creatures and spells to deal damage to the opponent as fast as possible. The deck focuses on using cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Emberheart Challenger to put pressure on the opponent while also using burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to remove any threats on the board. The deck also has some card draw with cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Academic Dispute to keep up the pressure and maintain card advantage.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on using efficient creatures and combat tricks to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By playing creatures like Archon of Emeria, Thalia, and Skyclave Apparition, along with Collected Company to find more threats, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain board presence. The inclusion of cards like Michelangelo's Technique and Abandoned Air Temple provide additional reach and ways to push through damage. With a mix of powerful creatures and disruptive elements, this deck looks to secure victories by staying ahead on board and tempo.

This deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creatures like Llanowar Elves and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, while disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent's defenses and secure a fast victory by playing powerful cards such as Collected Company and Ouroboroid. The inclusion of cards like Abandoned Air Temple and Boseiju, Who Endures provide additional utility and resilience to the deck, making it a formidable aggro strategy in the Pioneer 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on utilizing the Greasefang Parhelion combo to quickly generate a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. The key strategy involves using cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also utilizing Monument to Endurance to protect key creatures. The deck also includes various lands to ensure consistency in mana production and includes protection cards like Cling to Dust and Vanishing Verse to handle opposing threats. Overall, the deck aims to ramp up quickly and dominate the game with its powerful combo.

The deck focuses on controlling the board with cards like Cleansing Wildfire, Field of Ruin, and Abrade while also utilizing cards like Krenko's Buzzcrusher and Magmatic Hellkite to apply pressure. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value by flickering key cards for extra advantage. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Price of Freedom and Temporary Lockdown to thwart opponent's strategies. The overall strategy revolves around maintaining control of the game through removal, disruption, and powerful creatures.

This deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate a large amount of mana quickly, allowing for powerful combos such as Scapeshift to be played early on. The deck also includes cards like Lumra, Bellow of the Woods and Aftermath Analyst to provide additional support and card advantage. The goal is to establish a strong board presence while setting up for a game-ending combo play, overwhelming the opponent before they have a chance to react. By carefully managing resources and timing, this deck aims to achieve victory through explosive plays and strategic decision-making.

This deck focuses on quickly building a board presence and dealing damage to the opponent as efficiently as possible. It utilizes cheap spells and creatures to apply pressure early on, with the ability to control the opponent's board through bounce effects and removal spells. The key strategy is to keep the pressure on the opponent while maintaining card advantage and tempo, eventually overwhelming them with a combination of creatures and burn spells. The goal is to win the game as quickly as possible before the opponent can stabilize or set up their own game plan.
